untranslatable_reason: This signal requires detection of futures trading halts (limit up/down), options market activity status, and options-to-futures price derivation. None of these can be expressed using only the allowed price/technical indicators (OHLCV, moving averages, RSI, ATR, Bollinger Bands, MACD, ADX, Stochastic, Donchian, Pivots, volume ratios, and bar properties). The signal requires: (1) Market state detection (halt status) not available in price data, (2) Relative options-to-futures trading activity comparison, and (3) Implied pricing calculations from option Greeks/bid-ask spreads. This is a market microstructure and derivative pricing strategy that cannot be operationalized with technical indicators alone.
translation_notes: MCMILLAN_DRY_305 is an event-driven arbitrage signal exploiting temporary price discovery disconnects between halted futures and still-trading options. It requires real-time order book/exchange status data and options pricing models (implied volatility, option Greeks, put-call parity) rather than historical OHLCV indicators. Applicable to Chicago Merc currency options, Municipal Bond Index, Nikkei stock index, and silver futures.
